answer:
\boxed{A}

The **Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU)** defines the largest link-layer frame size that can be transmitted without fragmentation, which directly influences the **Maximum Segment Size (MSS)**—the largest TCP segment (IP payload) that can be encapsulated within that frame. The MSS is derived by subtracting TCP/IP header overhead from the MTU. Thus, the MTU is the foundational metric used to set the MSS.
